attributes.description: Alerts when the p99 range-slice latency exceeds 500 ms (`cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.99p > 500000 us`). Range scans are inherently expensive but sustained high p99 indicates trouble.
attributes.name: [Cassandra OTel] High range slice latency (p99)

investigation_guide.blob:
## Cassandra high range slice latency (p99)

### What fired
p99 of `cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.99p` exceeded 500 ms (500,000 µs) on at least one node.

### Why it matters
Range slices (used by secondary-index queries and full-table scans) are the most expensive read operation in Cassandra. Sustained high p99 indicates disk pressure, compaction backlog, or cross-DC queries; applications that depend on range scans will time out.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: confirm affected nodes and trend.
2. Correlate with compaction backlog (`cassandra.compaction.tasks.pending`) and read latency.
3. Look at disk I/O and page-cache pressure.
4. Review query patterns — range scans on non-partition keys are an antipattern and may need data modelling changes.

### Remediation
- Resolve compaction backlog.
- Move range-scan workloads to an analytics replica (Spark, Presto).
- Redesign the data model to support the query with a partition-scoped read.

### Tuning
- 500 ms is a reasonable default; adjust to the latency tolerance of the dependent application.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-cassandra.otel-default
// RangeSlice (range scans, secondary index) latency; inherently higher than single-partition reads
| WHERE `cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.99p` IS NOT NULL
| STATS p99_us = MAX(MAX_OVER_TIME(`cassandra.client.request.range_slice.latency.99p`)) BY server.address
// Threshold: 500000 us = 500 ms; range scans are more expensive
| WHERE p99_us > 500000
| SORT p99_us DESC
